questions.stem: Sociologists tend to define family in terms of

choices.label: A
choices.text: how a given society sanctions the relationships of people who are connected through blood, marriage, or adoption

choices.label: C
choices.text: the status roles that exist in a family structure

choices.label: D
choices.text: how closely members adhere to social norms

questions.explanation: Sociologists define family broadly in terms of how a society recognizes relationships among people connected by blood, marriage, or adoption, rather than by bloodline alone.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 14 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 14 Q1 = A.

questions.stem: A woman being married to two men would be an example of:
questions.explanation: Polyandry is the form of polygamy in which one woman has multiple husbands. (Polygyny is one man with multiple wives.)
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 14 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 14 Q3 = C.

questions.stem: Which of the following is a criticism of the family life cycle model?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is too broad and accounts for too many aspects of family.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is too narrowly focused on a sequence of stages.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It does not serve a practical purpose for studying family behavior.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is not based on comprehensive research.

questions.explanation: A common criticism is that the family life cycle model is too narrowly focused on a fixed sequence of stages, which does not fit the diversity of real families.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 14 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 14 Q5 = B.

questions.stem: According to the study cited by the U.S. Census Bureau, children who live with married parents grow up with more advantages than children who live with:
questions.explanation: Per the source, the cited Census Bureau study found that children with married parents tend to grow up with more advantages than children living with a single parent.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 14 Section Quiz

questions.stem: Same-sex couple households account for _____ percent of U.S. households.
questions.explanation: Per the source, same-sex couple households make up roughly 1 percent of U.S. households.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 14 Section Quiz

questions.explanation: Per the source, current U.S. divorce rates are steadily declining after peaking in earlier decades.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 14 Section Quiz

questions.stem: In general, children in ______ households benefit from divorce.
questions.explanation: Children in high-conflict households tend to benefit from divorce, because the divorce removes them from ongoing exposure to conflict.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 14 Section Quiz

questions.stem: Which type of child abuse is most prevalent in the United States?
questions.explanation: Neglect is the most prevalent form of child maltreatment reported in the United States.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 14 Section Quiz

questions.stem: In what ways does religion serve the role of a social institution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Religions have a complex and integrated set of norms.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Religious practices and beliefs are related to societal values.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Religions often meet several basic needs.

questions.explanation: Religion functions as a social institution in all of these ways: it has integrated norms, connects to societal values, and meets basic social needs.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 15 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q1 = D.

questions.stem: Which of the main theoretical perspectives would approach religion from the micro-level, studying how religion impacts an individual's sense of support and well-being?
questions.explanation: Symbolic interactionism works at the micro level, examining how religion shapes individuals' meanings, support, and well-being.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 15 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 15 Q3 = B.

questions.stem: Which sociological perspective most emphasizes the ways in which religion helps to maintain social inequalities within a society?
questions.explanation: Conflict theory emphasizes how religion can legitimize and maintain social inequalities (e.g., Marx's view of religion as the 'opium of the people').

questions.stem: The Protestant work ethic was viewed in terms of its relationship to:
questions.explanation: Max Weber linked the Protestant work ethic to the rise and spirit of capitalism.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 15 Section Quiz

choices.label: A
choices.text: has generally grown so large that it needs new buildings and multiple leaders

choices.label: B
choices.text: often believes it must split from the larger group to return to important fundamentals

choices.label: C
choices.text: is another term for a cult

questions.explanation: A sect is typically a smaller breakaway group that splits from a larger religious body to return to what it sees as essential fundamentals.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 15 Section Quiz

questions.stem: Some controversial groups that may be mislabeled as cults include:

choices.label: B
choices.text: the Peoples Temple and Heaven's Gate

choices.label: C
choices.text: the Branch Davidians and the Manson Family

questions.explanation: Per the source, groups such as Scientology and the Hare Krishna are sometimes mislabeled as cults, when sociologists would more neutrally call them new religious movements.

questions.stem: Many stories in the sacred text of Judaism are:

choices.label: B
choices.text: oral traditions only because Judaism has no sacred text

choices.label: D
choices.text: no longer part of the Torah

questions.explanation: As Abrahamic religions, Judaism, Christianity, and Islam share many of the same narratives and figures, so many stories in Judaism's sacred text are shared by Christianity and Islam.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 15 Section Quiz

questions.stem: Social scientists refer to the use of a church to combat social injustice in the political realm as:
questions.explanation: Liberation theology is the use of religious institutions and teachings to combat social and political injustice.
